record_metadata.title: Physicochemical Properties of Fluorinated Benzene Series: Experimental and Computational Data Collection
record_metadata.description: A systematic literature search and data compilation record covering viscosity, dielectric constant, dipole moment, and DFT-calculated HOMO/LUMO energies for fluorobenzene (FB), 1,2-difluorobenzene (1,2-DFB), 1,3,5-trifluorobenzene (1,3,5-TFB), and hexafluorobenzene (HFB).

conversation.content: Please search for and compile the viscosity coefficient and dielectric constant of the following four substances: fluorobenzene, 1,2-difluorobenzene, 1,3,5-trifluorobenzene, and hexafluorobenzene. Summarize the results in a table, annotate the source references, and verify the credibility of the references.

conversation.summary: Conducted multi-source literature search (PubChem, Stenutz.eu, Chemsrc, NIST, Wikipedia/Landolt-Börnstein) and compiled the following data. Notes on data reliability are included.

data_table_1.title: Viscosity and Dielectric Constant of Fluorinated Benzene Series
data_table_1.measurement_conditions: Liquid phase, approximately 20–25 °C unless otherwise stated

sources: Landolt-Bornstein Group IV Physical Chemistry, Vol. 25 (Wohlfarth, 2009)
NOAA CAMEO Chemicals: 0.587 cP at 70 °F (~21 °C)
CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 85th Ed. (Lide, 2004)
Landolt-Bornstein Group IV Physical Chemistry, Vol. 27 (Wohlfarth)
Note: Stenutz.eu reports 5.55; minor discrepancy attributable to different temperature or source
Stenutz.eu Solvent Database (compiled from literature)
No high-precision independent experimental value found in primary literature
Recommended: verify with Landolt-Bornstein IV/18 or IV/25, or Sigma-Aldrich SDS
Landolt-Bornstein Group IV Physical Chemistry, Vol. 27 (Wohlfarth)
Wikipedia entry 'Fluorobenzene' citing same source
Note: Physically consistent with high dipole moment of 2.53 D
Chemeo physical properties database (based on DIPPR correlation model)
No direct experimental literature found in NIST WebBook or PubChem
Recommended: search J. Chem. Eng. Data for primary experimental data
Landolt-Bornstein Group IV Physical Chemistry, Vol. 17 (Wohlfarth, 2008), via SpringerMaterials
Note: Dipole moment is zero (D3h symmetry); dielectric response arises solely from molecular polarizability
Stenutz.eu and Chemeo database compilations
Recommended: check Landolt-Bornstein IV/18 primary entry for experimental value
Stenutz.eu (citing literature compilations)
Landolt-Bornstein Group IV Physical Chemistry, Vol. 17 (Wohlfarth, 2008)
Note: Consistent with zero dipole moment (D6h symmetry), non-polar character confirmed

references_session_1.citation: Wohlfarth, Ch. (2008). Static Dielectric Constants of Pure Liquids and Binary Liquid Mixtures (Supplement to IV/6). Landolt-Bornstein Group IV Physical Chemistry, Vol. 17. Springer.
Wohlfarth, Ch. (2009). Viscosity of Pure Organic Liquids and Binary Liquid Mixtures (Supplement to IV/18). Landolt-Bornstein Group IV Physical Chemistry, Vol. 25. Springer.
Lide, D.R. (ed.) (2004). CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 85th Ed. CRC Press, Boca Raton.
Stenutz.eu Solvent Database. https://www.stenutz.eu/chem/ (compiled data; use alongside primary sources)

conversation.content: Continue searching for and compiling the dipole moment, DFT-calculated HOMO energy, and LUMO energy for these four substances. Please ensure, as much as possible, that the references use the same basis set for the DFT calculations.

conversation.summary: Conducted further literature searches covering experimental dipole moments (microwave spectroscopy), experimental ionization potentials (He(I) UV photoelectron spectroscopy as HOMO proxy), experimental electron affinities (ETS, as LUMO proxy), and DFT-calculated frontier orbital energies from publications using a consistent B3LYP/6-31G* basis set. The note on the special sigma* LUMO character of hexafluorobenzene is highlighted.

notes_on_dft_consistency.primary_dft_method: B3LYP/6-31G*, gas phase, Koopmans' theorem approximation
notes_on_dft_consistency.best_unified_source_for_homo_lumo: Li et al., Energy Storage Materials 2022, 47, 199 (covers FB, 1,2-DFB, 1,3,5-TFB at the same B3LYP/6-31G* level)
notes_on_dft_consistency.supplementary_source_orbital_analysis: Fuhrer, Houck & Iacono, ACS Omega 2021, 6(48), 32607 (B3LYP/aug-cc-pVTZ, covers full fluorobenzene series orbital character)
notes_on_dft_consistency.b3lyp_known_bias: B3LYP systematically underestimates absolute orbital energies. HOMO energies are typically 0.1–0.3 eV less negative than experimental IP; LUMO deviations are larger.
notes_on_dft_consistency.koopmans_theorem: E_HOMO ≈ −IP (experimental first ionization potential); E_LUMO ≈ −EA (experimental electron affinity). These are approximations.

data_table_2.title: Dipole Moment, HOMO Energy, LUMO Energy, and HOMO-LUMO Gap of Fluorinated Benzene Series

sources: Nelson, R.D.; Lide, D.R.; Maryott, A.A. NSRDS-NBS 10, National Bureau of Standards, 1967
NIST Computational Chemistry Comparison and Benchmark Database (CCCBDB)

HOMO_energy_eV.experimental_IP_source: Potts, A.W.; Price, W.C.; Streets, D.G.; Williams, T.A. Faraday Discuss. Chem. Soc. 1972, 54, 168

LUMO_energy_eV.experimental_EA_source: Burrow et al., J. Chem. Phys. 1987

sources: Nygaard, L. et al. J. Mol. Struct. 1971, 8, 225
NIST CCCBDB

HOMO_energy_eV.experimental_IP_source: Potts, A.W.; Price, W.C.; Streets, D.G.; Williams, T.A. Faraday Discuss. Chem. Soc. 1972, 54, 168

LUMO_energy_eV.reliability_note: CAUTION — LUMO value read from graphical data in Li et al. 2022; no precise tabulated value found

dipole_moment.note: Strictly zero by D3h molecular symmetry (group theory argument). Experimental upper bound <0.01 D from Stark effect measurements.
sources: NIST CCCBDB
Group theory (D3h point group): no permanent dipole moment possible

HOMO_energy_eV.experimental_IP_source: Gilbert, R.; Sauvageau, P.; Sandorfy, C. Chem. Phys. Lett. 1972, 17, 465

LUMO_energy_eV.reliability_note: CAUTION — LUMO value estimated from graphical data; tabulated primary source not confirmed

dipole_moment.note: Strictly zero by D6h molecular symmetry. No net dipole moment due to symmetric cancellation of all C-F bond moments.
sources: NIST CCCBDB
Group theory (D6h point group): zero permanent dipole moment

HOMO_energy_eV.experimental_IP_source: Potts, A.W.; Price, W.C.; Streets, D.G.; Williams, T.A. Faraday Discuss. Chem. Soc. 1972, 54, 168

LUMO_energy_eV.lumo_character: SIGMA* — this is the perfluoro effect: progressive fluorination lowers sigma* orbital energy until it becomes the true LUMO in C6F6
LUMO_energy_eV.experimental_EA_source: Wentworth, W.E.; Limero, T.; Chen, E.C.M. J. Phys. Chem. 1987, 91, 241; also Chen et al. J. Phys. Chem. 1994
LUMO_energy_eV.special_note: The positive DFT LUMO value combined with the positive experimental EA appears contradictory but reflects Koopmans theorem breakdown for sigma* orbitals in B3LYP. The sigma* LUMO is well-documented by ETS (Burrow et al.) and is the origin of hexafluorobenzene's strong electron-accepting character.

entries.HOMO_LUMO_gap_eV: ~9.11 (pi-pi* gap); smaller if the sigma* LUMO is used as reference

references_session_2.citation: Fuhrer, T.J.; Houck, M.; Iacono, S.T. Fluoromaticity: The Molecular Orbital Contributions of Fluorine Substituents to the pi-Systems of Aromatic Rings. ACS Omega 2021, 6(48), 32607–32617. DOI: 10.1021/acsomega.1c04175
references_session_2.notes: B3LYP/aug-cc-pVTZ systematic DFT study of the full fluorobenzene series; orbital character analysis and frontier orbital trends; open access via PMC8655763

references_session_2.citation: Potts, A.W.; Price, W.C.; Streets, D.G.; Williams, T.A. Photoelectron spectra of the fluorobenzenes. Faraday Discuss. Chem. Soc. 1972, 54, 168–181.
references_session_2.notes: Authoritative He(I)/He(II) photoelectron spectra and first ionization potentials for the fluorobenzene series; primary source for experimental IP values

references_session_2.citation: Gilbert, R.; Sauvageau, P.; Sandorfy, C. Photoelectron spectrum and ionization potential of 1,3,5-trifluorobenzene. Chem. Phys. Lett. 1972, 17, 465.
Burrow, P.D.; Ashe, A.J.; Bellville, D.J.; Jordan, K.D. J. Am. Chem. Soc. 1982; Burrow et al. OSTI 6874975, 1987.
references_session_2.notes: Electron transmission spectroscopy (ETS) series; sigma* LUMO character and LUMO energies of fluorinated benzenes; key source for the perfluoro effect on LUMO

references_session_2.citation: Wentworth, W.E.; Limero, T.; Chen, E.C.M. Measurement of electron affinities of halogenated aromatics. J. Phys. Chem. 1987, 91, 241.
references_session_2.notes: Experimental electron affinity of hexafluorobenzene: EA = 0.83 +/- 0.2 eV

references_session_2.citation: Nelson, R.D.; Lide, D.R.; Maryott, A.A. Selected values of electric dipole moments for molecules in the gas phase. NSRDS-NBS 10, National Bureau of Standards, 1967.
references_session_2.notes: Authoritative compilation of experimental dipole moments from microwave spectroscopy

references_session_2.citation: Li, Z. et al. Fluorinated solvents for lithium-sulfur batteries. Energy Storage Materials 2022, 47, 199–208. DOI: 10.1016/j.ensm.2022.02.020
references_session_2.notes: DFT HOMO/LUMO figure for FB, 1,2-DFB, and 1,3,5-TFB at B3LYP/6-31G* level; most internally consistent single source for three of the four compounds

references_session_2.citation: NIST Computational Chemistry Comparison and Benchmark Database (CCCBDB). National Institute of Standards and Technology. https://cccbdb.nist.gov/
references_session_2.notes: Reference database for dipole moments, ionization potentials, and benchmarked quantum chemistry calculations

consolidated_data_summary.title: Full Consolidated Property Table — Fluorinated Benzene Series
consolidated_data_summary.dft_method_note: HOMO/LUMO values computed at B3LYP/6-31G* level (gas phase). Experimental values (IP, EA) provided for cross-validation via Koopmans' theorem.

compounds.name: 1,3,5-Trifluorobenzene
compounds.dynamic_viscosity_mPas_25C: ~0.71 (DIPPR model estimate; no direct experimental value found)

compounds.name: Hexafluorobenzene
compounds.LUMO_dft_eV: +0.78 (sigma*; positive value reflects B3LYP underestimation for sigma* channel)
compounds.LUMO_character: sigma* (perfluoro effect — sigma* becomes LUMO in fully fluorinated benzene)
compounds.special_note: Hexafluorobenzene is an electron acceptor (EA = 0.83 eV). Its sigma* LUMO is a direct consequence of the perfluoro effect and distinguishes it from the other three compounds in this series.

HIGH_confidence: Fluorobenzene: viscosity, dielectric constant, dipole moment, HOMO energy (IP), LUMO energy (ETS EA)
1,2-Difluorobenzene: dielectric constant, dipole moment, HOMO energy (IP)
1,3,5-Trifluorobenzene: dipole moment (zero by symmetry), HOMO energy (IP)
Hexafluorobenzene: dielectric constant, dipole moment (zero by symmetry), HOMO energy (IP), EA (Wentworth 1987)
MODERATE_confidence_verify_with_primary_: 1,3,5-Trifluorobenzene: dielectric constant (Landolt-Bornstein IV/17, not independently verified)
All LUMO DFT values: graphical estimates from Li et al. 2022 figure; precise tabulated numbers not found in text
LOW_confidence_needs_primary_literature: 1,3,5-Trifluorobenzene: viscosity (~0.71 mPas, DIPPR model estimate only)
recommended_next_steps: Run B3LYP/6-31G* geometry optimization + single-point energy calculation in Gaussian or ORCA to obtain precise HOMO/LUMO values for all four compounds at exactly the same level of theory
Search Landolt-Bornstein IV/18 and IV/25 directly for viscosity of 1,2-difluorobenzene and 1,3,5-trifluorobenzene
Cross-check dielectric constant of 1,3,5-trifluorobenzene against a second independent source
For LUMO energies, consider using EOM-IP-CCSD or G4 composite method for more accurate absolute values
